doc_title: InMoDeGAN: Interpretable Motion Decomposition Generative Adversarial Network for Video Generation

items.evidence_span: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) [13]
items.relevance: Identifying potential starting point of the citation chain

items.evidence_span: We implement InMoDeGAN using PyTorch [35].
We use Adam optimizer [23] with a learning rate 0.002 and set $\beta_{1}=0.0, \beta_{2}=0.99$.

We construct a subset of VoxCeleb2 [30], which comprises of over 1 million videos pertaining to 6000 celebrities, talking in different real-world scenarios containing diverse complex motions (e.g., head moving, talking, camera zooming, etc.).
We use video FID [15] to quantitatively evaluate visual quality and temporal consistency in generated videos.
For the computation, we appropriate ResNeXt-101 [14] pre-trained on Kinetics-400 [6] as feature extractor and take features before last fully connected layer to compute the FID.
For the computation, we appropriate ResNeXt-101 [14] pre-trained on Kinetics-400 [6] as feature extractor and take features before last fully connected layer to compute the FID.
Based on the idea of equivariance [25,9,16], an alteration in the latent space causes a corresponding alteration in the output
Recent works [18,39] showed that for a given imagetransformation $\mathcal{T}$ such as shifting and zooming, there exists a vector $d$ in the latent space
We adapt the architecture proposed by Karras et al. [22] in $G_{S}$.
Temporal speed in videos has been a pertinent cue in action recognition [11,56].
Principally, our discriminator follows the two-stream architecture of MoCoGAN [42] and $\mathrm{G}^{3} \mathrm{AN}$ [52].
Principally, our discriminator follows the two-stream architecture of MoCoGAN [42] and $\mathrm{G}^{3} \mathrm{AN}$ [52].
We use non-saturating loss [13] with $\mathcal{R}_{1}$ regularization [28, 22] as our objective function following the setting of StyleGAN2 [22].
We use non-saturating loss [13] with $\mathcal{R}_{1}$ regularization [28, 22] as our objective function following the setting of StyleGAN2 [22].
we generate $128 \times 128$ videos, as trained on VoxCeleb2-mini, as well as on UCF101 [40].
items.relevance: potential link in the citation chain

items.evidence_span: we conduct quantitative evaluation based on a metric proposed by TGANv2 [37].
